growth_orientation_rationale: The rapid use of EU funds helped mitigate the negative impacts of fiscal tightening by boosting investment and supporting startups, which can enhance economic growth.
status_evidence: The total amount of EU grants increased significantly during the recession.
importance_rationale: The acceleration of EU fund utilization represents a significant response to economic challenges, enhancing support for startups and investment during a critical period.
description: Estonia accelerated the use of EU structural funds to support investment and employment during the recession, increasing the total amount of EU grants from 2.2% of GDP in 2008 to 5.1% of GDP in 2009.
source_quote: The total amount of the EU grants increased from 2.2% of GDP in 2008 to 5.1% of GDP in 2009.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating connections between SMEs and research institutions, innovation vouchers enhance the development of new products and services, thereby supporting economic growth.
status_evidence: Innovation vouchers have been utilized to assist SMEs in establishing contacts with universities.
importance_rationale: The programme provides a significant tool for SMEs to access expertise and support for innovation, contributing to the overall innovation ecosystem.
description: Innovation vouchers have been in place since 2009 to help SMEs connect with universities and experts for developing innovative solutions.
source_quote: Innovation vouchers in place since 2009 help SMEs establish their first contact with universities or other experts to develop innovative solutions.

growth_orientation_rationale: The voucher scheme increases the relevance of training for the unemployed, thereby improving their employability and addressing skills mismatches in the labor market.
status_evidence: The scheme is expected to continue in 2011 with increased funding and expanded scope.
importance_rationale: The voucher scheme represents a significant enhancement of existing training measures, targeting skills mismatches and improving labor market outcomes for the unemployed.
description: Estonia has implemented a pilot voucher scheme allowing the unemployed to choose relevant training programs, with funding increased for 2011 to enhance its scope and effectiveness in addressing skills mismatches in the labor market.
source_quote: Another useful innovation was the adoption from October 2009 of a pilot voucher scheme, which allows the unemployed, together with a PES consultant, to choose a relevant training scheme up to a cost of EUR1000.

growth_orientation_rationale: The scheme aims to reduce unemployment by fostering entrepreneurship, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The scheme is currently operational and providing support to unemployed individuals.
importance_rationale: The start-up scheme represents a significant effort to address unemployment through entrepreneurship, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation ecosystem.
description: Estonia has introduced a business start-up scheme that provides unemployed individuals with a start-up grant, business training, and ongoing mentor support to encourage entrepreneurship.
source_quote: The range of activation measures has also been expanded by offering a business start-up scheme.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing vocational training and qualifications, the reform aims to improve the employability of students and meet labor market needs, thus supporting economic growth.
status_evidence: Grants for vocational students were introduced to support their education and training.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances vocational education opportunities, which are crucial for aligning skills with labor market demands.
description: Grants for vocational students were introduced to support the acquisition of vocational education and training abroad, aimed at improving qualifications and facilitating technology transfer.
source_quote: Grants for vocational students could also support the acquisition of vocational education and training abroad.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The target for R&D spending was set as part of the national strategy.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding, aiming to shift the balance towards private sector contributions and enhance overall innovation capacity.
description: Estonia aims to raise its R&D spending from 1.5% of GDP in 2008 to 3% by 2014, with a focus on increasing private sector contributions to R&D funding.
source_quote: Knowledge-based Estonia’ set a target of raising spending on research and development from 1½ per cent of GDP in 2008 to just under 2% by 2010 and 3% by 2014.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving R&D infrastructure and researcher compensation is expected to boost research output and innovation capacity.
status_evidence: Co-ordinated large-scale investments are being carried out over the 2008-15 period.
importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical deficiencies in the R&D framework, aiming for a comprehensive upgrade of research capabilities.
description: The Estonian government is modernising R&D infrastructure and increasing pay for researchers to enhance the quality and quantity of R&D personnel.
source_quote: Measures are being taken to enhance the quality and numbers of people working in research and development through increased pay and the modernisation of R&D infrastructure.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between academia and industry, enhancing innovation capacity and applied research outcomes.
status_evidence: The government has established development vouchers to promote cooperation.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration between research and business sectors, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
description: The Estonian government has introduced development vouchers to incentivize cooperation between research institutions and businesses, aiming to strengthen knowledge transfer and applied research.
source_quote: Development vouchers incentivising co-operation between research institutions and businesses have been introduced in 2015.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance productivity and innovation by addressing real-life problems and fostering collaboration between universities and industries.
package_name: Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20
status_evidence: The strategy set a clear target for R&D spending and aimed to reverse the decline in funding.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Estonia's approach to R&D funding, aiming to double spending and enhance the link between research and practical applications.
description: The Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20 aims to increase R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2020, reversing a decline in funding and promoting industry involvement in research.
source_quote: The Estonian Research, Development and Innovation Strategy 2014-20 set a target for R&D spending of 3% of GDP by 2020.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance the innovation capacity of firms, leading to increased productivity and competitiveness.
status_evidence: Enterprise Estonia has been actively providing support since 2020.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for applied research, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the private sector.
description: Since 2020, Enterprise Estonia has been offering both consulting and financial support for conducting applied research, aimed at enhancing innovation capabilities among businesses.
source_quote: For innovation, since 2020 Enterprise Estonia offers both consulting and financial support for conducting applied research.

growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning research with the specific needs of industries, this initiative is expected to enhance the relevance and application of research outcomes, driving economic growth.
status_evidence: The Ministry has initiated these programmes based on the needs of entrepreneurs.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses the gap between research and market needs, fostering innovation in key sectors.
description: The Ministry of Education and Research has initiated sectoral research programmes tailored to the needs of entrepreneurs, aiming to align research efforts with market demands.
source_quote: The Ministry of Education and Research has also initiated sectoral research programmes based on the needs of entrepreneurs.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D investment is expected to enhance innovation in low-carbon technologies, which can lead to sustainable economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has announced plans to expand public R&D investment.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards green technology, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering innovation.
description: Estonia is expanding public R&D investment and increasing funding for environment-related issues to encourage low-carbon technology innovation.
source_quote: Further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and by increasing the share of funding on environment-related issues.

growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives for R&D are expected to stimulate private sector investment in innovation, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: Consideration should be given to introducing tax incentives for R&D.
importance_rationale: The introduction of tax incentives for R&D represents a significant shift in the funding approach for innovation, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of existing support mechanisms.
description: Estonia is considering introducing tax incentives for R&D to enhance support for innovation and globalisation activities, which are currently primarily funded through EU structural funds and grants.
source_quote: In order to avoid a not very promising 'picking winners' strategy, more consideration should be given to introducing tax incentives for R&D.

growth_orientation_rationale: By financing R&D and innovation, the plan aims to enhance the technological capabilities of firms, leading to improved productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The measures are part of the Estonian National Development Plan.
importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant commitment to enhancing R&D and innovation, although they are part of a broader strategic framework.
description: The Estonian National Development Plan includes measures aimed at financing R&D and innovation, strengthening innovation systems, and increasing awareness about innovation.
source_quote: EAS is the implementing body for the technology and innovation measures of the Estonian National Development Plan.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to training for low productivity workers, the reform aims to enhance skills and employability, thereby improving overall productivity.
status_evidence: The plan to extend the training voucher scheme is discussed as a potential measure.
importance_rationale: The extension of the training voucher scheme represents a significant effort to improve workforce skills, particularly for underrepresented groups, which is crucial for economic growth.
description: Estonia plans to extend the training voucher scheme towards employees, financing it through contributions from employers and employees, as well as direct public support in areas with evidence of underinvestment.
source_quote: Estonia could extend the training voucher scheme towards employees and finance them through employers and employees’ contributions, but also by direct public support in areas where there is evidence of underinvestment.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination can lead to more effective implementation of innovation policies, fostering a better environment for R&D activities.
status_evidence: Responsibilities for R&D policies have been clarified and coordination across ministries has been strengthened.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances the governance of R&D policies, which is crucial for effective innovation management.
description: The Estonian government has clarified responsibilities for R&D policies and strengthened coordination across ministries to enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies.
source_quote: Responsibilities for R&D policies have been clarified and coordination across ministries has been strengthened.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing knowledge transfer is expected to boost innovation and productivity in domestic firms, leading to economic growth.
status_evidence: Plans to promote applied research and improve collaboration have been outlined.
importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant gap in collaboration between firms and research institutions, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
description: To strengthen knowledge transfers to domestic firms, Estonia plans to promote applied research and improve collaboration with domestic and foreign institutions conducting applied research.
source_quote: To strengthen knowledge transfers to domestic firms, promote applied research and improve collaboration with domestic and foreign institutions conducting applied research.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the evaluation of innovation policies can lead to more effective resource allocation and better outcomes for innovation initiatives.
status_evidence: Evaluations have been taken into account in the design of measures to support innovation.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant improvement in the governance of innovation policies, which can enhance their effectiveness and impact on the economy.
description: Estonia is enhancing the effectiveness of innovation policies by incorporating monitoring and evaluation at the design stage, which includes pilot projects for individual instruments.
source_quote: Enhance effectiveness of evaluation of innovation policies by incorporating monitoring and evaluation already at the design stage.

growth_orientation_rationale: By stimulating demand for innovative solutions, the programme can enhance productivity and encourage firms to innovate.
status_evidence: A demand-side public procurement programme is in place.
importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to leverage public procurement for innovation, which can have broader implications for the economy.
description: Estonia has implemented a demand-side public procurement programme aimed at fostering innovation through public sector purchases, although initial take-up has been low.
source_quote: A demand-side public procurement programme is in place, and although it may be too early to evaluate its reach, the take-up so far has been low.

growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging collaboration between public R&D institutions and the private sector can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The recommendation to give more weight to private sector cooperation was made as part of the key recommendations.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding allocation that could enhance the effectiveness of public R&D by fostering private sector collaboration.
description: Estonia aims to enhance knowledge diffusion by giving more weight to cooperation with the private sector when allocating funds to public R&D institutions.
source_quote: Give more weight to cooperation with the private sector when allocating funds to public R&D institutions.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is likely to enhance innovation capabilities, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: Planned increases in government spending on research and development are expected to boost innovation economy-wide.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Estonia, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and competitiveness.
description: The Estonian government plans to increase spending on research and development, which is expected to boost innovation across the economy.
source_quote: Planned increases in government spending on research and development are expected to boost innovation economy-wide.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D investment in low-carbon technologies is expected to stimulate innovation, enhance productivity, and support the transition to a sustainable economy.
status_evidence: The strategy to expand public R&D investment was announced as part of the efforts to transition to a low-carbon economy.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards environmental issues, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering innovation in low-carbon technologies.
description: Estonia aims to further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and increasing the share of funding directed towards environment-related issues.
source_quote: Further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and by increasing the share of funding on environment-related issues.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing managerial practices can lead to improved organizational performance, which in turn supports innovation and productivity in firms.
package_name: Estonian Research and Development, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Plan 2021-2035
status_evidence: Programmes and activities targeted to disseminate good practice and mutual learning will be continued and developed.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the capabilities of firms through improved management, which is crucial for fostering innovation and competitiveness.
description: Estonia is implementing a programme to improve managerial practices and organizational performance of firms, which includes a strong element of network-building to disseminate good practices and mutual learning.
source_quote: Improvement of managerial practices and organizational performance of firms is one of the action targeted throughout Estonian Research and Development, Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Plan 2021-2035.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding will enhance innovation in low-carbon technologies, contributing to environmental sustainability and economic growth.
status_evidence: Estonia should further develop its innovation capacity by increasing overall public R&D funding.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the need for increased funding in a specific area, but it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
description: Estonia aims to increase overall public R&D funding and shift its focus towards environment-related issues to support the transition to a low-carbon economy.
source_quote: To support the transition to a low-carbon economy, Estonia should further develop its innovation capacity by increasing overall public R&D funding.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative supports the transition to cleaner fuels in public transport, which can reduce emissions and improve public health, contributing to overall economic growth.
status_evidence: Estonia has been providing subsidies and grants since 2015.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant investment in cleaner public transport, which has implications for environmental sustainability and public health.
description: Estonia has provided subsidies to establish biomethane filling stations and awarded grants to public bus operators that use biomethane as a fuel, promoting the use of cleaner energy in public transport.
source_quote: Estonia has provided subsidies to establish biomethane filling stations and has awarded grants to public bus operators that use it as a fuel.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to digital tools, the program aims to enhance productivity and competitiveness among firms, particularly SMEs.
status_evidence: The program is currently operational, providing grants to eligible firms.
importance_rationale: While the program is significant in supporting digital transformation, its current limitations on eligibility reduce its overall impact on the broader SME sector.
description: The Estonian Business and Innovation Agency offers grants to cover the costs of digital diagnostics, aimed at enhancing the digital capabilities of firms. However, the program is currently limited to firms with annual sales above EUR 200,000, which restricts access for many SMEs.
source_quote: The Estonian Business and Innovation Agency offers grants to cover the costs of digital diagnostics.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration between businesses and research institutions is expected to enhance innovation and R&D outcomes.
status_evidence: A new centre for applied research aims to bring together the business and research sectors.
importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it seeks to bridge the gap between academia and industry, fostering innovation through collaboration.
description: A new centre for applied research aims to enhance collaboration between the business and research sectors, promoting innovation and R&D investments.
source_quote: A new centre for applied research aims to bring together the business and research sectors, which is welcome.

growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the science park facilitated collaboration between research institutions and industry, leading to innovation and economic growth in the telecommunications sector.
status_evidence: The telecommunications industry emerged from an initiative in the Oulu science park.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the Oulu science park represents a significant structural change in Finland's approach to fostering innovation and industry collaboration, with lasting impacts on the economy.
description: The Oulu science park, established in the early 1990s, played a crucial role in the emergence of the telecommunications industry in Finland, particularly with Nokia.
source_quote: The booming telecommunications industry (Nokia) emerged from an initiative in the Oulu science park in the early 1990s.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the effectiveness of public funding for research can lead to increased innovation and productivity in small and medium-sized enterprises.
status_evidence: The proposal was enacted as part of the 1995 Budget.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in how public support is evaluated and targeted, which can enhance the impact of funding on innovation.
description: The 1995 Budget proposal includes new initiatives aimed at improving the quality of existing support programmes, focusing on rigorous cost-benefit analysis and targeting subsidies on high social return projects, particularly in research for small and medium-sized enterprises.
source_quote: New initiatives announced in the 1995 Budget proposal focus on improving the quality of existing support programmes rather than cutting overall subsidy levels.

growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning education with labor market demands, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and employment opportunities.
package_name: 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research
status_evidence: Measures were announced in the context of the 1995 Development Plan.
importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant deficiencies in vocational education, aiming to improve alignment with labor market needs, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
description: The 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research includes measures to streamline vocational schools and develop a system of higher vocational education, aiming to improve the responsiveness of education to labor market needs.
source_quote: A number of measures announced in the context of the 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research are intended to resolve such weaknesses.

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to SMEs, the reform aims to enhance their technological capabilities, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
status_evidence: The Finnish Industrial Investment Company was established and is operational.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the Finnish Industrial Investment Company represents a significant step in leveraging privatisation proceeds for enhancing SME capabilities, which is crucial for economic growth.
description: The Finnish government established the Finnish Industrial Investment Company in February 1995 to allocate 'soft' loans to SMEs, funded by privatisation proceeds, aimed at strengthening industrial technology and know-how.
source_quote: In May 1996 the government has decided to use part of the privatisation proceeds to strengthen industrial technology and know-how, in particular in Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs).

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing risk capital and reducing administrative burdens, the measures are designed to facilitate the growth of innovative SMEs.
status_evidence: The measures have been actively implemented to support SMEs.
importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant but narrower reform aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem.
description: The Finnish government has introduced measures targeting small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), including the provision of risk capital and simpler administrative procedures to support their growth and innovation.
source_quote: Other measures have targeted small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), including the provision of risk capital and the introduction of simpler administrative procedures.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance support for R&D and product development, which are critical for innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The legislation was effective from January 1998.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in Finland's industrial policy towards supporting intangible investments.
description: The new legislation on industrial subsidies, effective January 1998, prioritizes support for research, product development, and training, marking a shift towards intangible investments in Finland's industrial policy. Additionally, the establishment of the 'Advisory Committee on Business Subsidies' aims to enhance the effectiveness of future support programmes while limiting public support to the manufacturing industry, ensuring that government measures correct market failures.
source_quote: The new legislation on general conditions for the provision of industrial subsidies, which entered into force in January 1998, states that support schemes shall be primarily targeted on research, product development, training or on some other form of immaterial corporate development.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The target of R&D spending was achieved in 1998, indicating successful implementation.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in R&D funding policy, significantly impacting the overall research landscape.
description: The Finnish government has significantly increased R&D support, utilizing part of the privatisation proceeds to achieve a target of R&D spending of 2.9% of GDP by 1999, which was successfully reached. This initiative has contributed to a rise in total research and development spending, positioning Finland among the highest in the OECD.
source_quote: In September 1996, the government decided to use part of the privatisation proceeds to foster R&D and it set a target of R&D spending of 2.9 per cent of GDP by 1999 – one of the highest ratios in the OECD area.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the transfer of innovations from research to industry is expected to stimulate economic growth and innovation.
component_name: Public R&D Support for Knowledge Transfer
status_evidence: The government has successfully implemented the channeling of public R&D support through designated councils and centres.
importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the mechanisms for knowledge transfer but does not fundamentally change the overall system.
description: The Finnish government has been channeling public R&D support primarily through research councils of the Academy of Finland and technology development centres (TEKES), which aim to enhance the transfer of innovations and new technology from universities and public research centres to companies.
source_quote: Public R&D support is mainly channelled through research councils of the Academy of Finland and through technology development centres (TEKES) of the Ministry of Trade and Industry.

growth_orientation_rationale: The network aims to enhance collaboration across sectors, which can foster innovation and improve biotechnology outcomes.
status_evidence: The network was established and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: The establishment of a cross-ministerial network is a significant but narrower reform aimed at addressing specific issues in biotechnology.
description: A network was established in October 2001 involving all ministries to address biotechnology issues across various sectors including science, innovation, and health.
source_quote: In October 2001 a network was established including all ministries with the aim of tackling biotechnology issues in science and innovation but also industry, environment, agriculture and forestry, health and trade policies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is expected to enhance productivity and support innovation in the ICT sector.
status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented as of 2002.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant increase in public R&D funding, which is crucial for supporting innovation.
description: Finland's government has increased its R&D spending, which is already high by international standards, to further support the development of the ICT sector and enhance productivity growth.
source_quote: Government R&D spending is also high by international standards, about 0.4 per cent of GDP in 2000.

growth_orientation_rationale: The project aims to enhance healthcare services, which can lead to improved public health and productivity.
status_evidence: The project has been initiated and is currently in force.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant policy change with substantial funding and a focus on health sector improvement.
description: The Finnish government has initiated the National Health Project aimed at improving the availability of health care, reducing waiting times, and enhancing staff training, with significant funding allocated for its implementation.
source_quote: The government has recently embarked on a reform of the health system, run by the Ministry of Social Affairs and Health and known as the National Health Project.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving vocational training and addressing skill shortages enhances the workforce's capabilities, which supports economic growth.
status_evidence: The performance-based financing for vocational training has been in effect since 2006, and the Migration Policy Programme is actively promoting work-related immigration.
importance_rationale: The reform significantly impacts the labor market and education system, representing a structural change in how vocational training is financed and how immigration policy addresses skill shortages.
description: Finland's Migration Policy Programme, implemented in 2006, aims to promote work-related immigration to address skill shortages by simplifying the permit system for guest workers and improving recognition of overseas qualifications. Additionally, a performance-based financing system was introduced to enhance the quality of vocational training through quantitative indicators and performance targets.
source_quote: A promising innovation, performance-based financing, was introduced in 2006. The system is based on quantitative indicators, with performance targets grouped into the categories of impact, process efficiency, and staff.

growth_orientation_rationale: Redirecting R&D spending towards non-technological areas is expected to foster innovation in public services and enhance overall productivity.
status_evidence: The Innovation Strategy was introduced and has been in effect since 2008.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to public R&D spending.
description: The Finnish government introduced a new Innovation Strategy in 2008, which aims to redirect public R&D spending towards non-technological areas such as public services and private sector innovation, enhancing cooperation between public and private sectors.
source_quote: The government introduced a new Innovation Strategy in 2008, which includes funnelling a larger share of public R&D spending towards non-technological areas such as the public sector and private services.

growth_orientation_rationale: Maintaining strong support for basic R&D is essential for enhancing the quality of research output, which contributes to long-term economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has implemented plans to distribute research grants based on academic performance.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in the allocation of public R&D funding.
description: The Finnish government plans to maintain strong support for basic R&D and education by distributing research grants based on academic performance, while also streamlining support for businesses and private sector R&D to focus on academic environments that reward quality.
source_quote: Current support for businesses and private sector R&D do not seem to be effective and should be scaled back further and streamlined together with business taxation.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving employment opportunities for immigrants is expected to enhance the labor market and contribute to economic growth.
status_evidence: The strategy has been announced but is not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: This strategy is significant for labor market integration but does not represent a comprehensive reform.
description: Finland has launched the Future of Migration 2020 Strategy to enhance immigration policies, aiming to improve employment opportunities for immigrants and integrate them into the labor market.
source_quote: Finland has already launched several programmes to promote immigration and enhance integration, notably the Future of Migration 2020 Strategy.

growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on green innovation and clean technologies is expected to drive economic growth and sustainability.
status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is actively being implemented.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at promoting green innovation on a large scale.
description: The Finnish government has launched an ambitious programme to promote green innovation, targeting EUR50 billion in the cleantech sector by 2020, in partnership with public and private stakeholders. This initiative is supported by various policies aimed at fostering innovation in clean technologies, which is expected to enhance green growth.
source_quote: Finland is a strong innovator in clean technologies... to accelerate the move towards green growth, the government has launched an ambitious programme to promote green innovation.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the funding model, the reform aims to improve research quality and relevance, which can lead to better economic outcomes.
status_evidence: The revised funding model has been put into practice and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the funding model for universities, impacting the entire academic research landscape.
description: The Finnish government has revised the university funding model to allocate research grants based on performance, aiming to improve academic outcomes and accelerate the transition to working life. This approach emphasizes the importance of monitoring direct public funding to ensure efficient resource allocation, particularly for basic research and education.
source_quote: Continue to support innovation using a broad approach. Monitor the impact of direct public funding to ensure efficient resource allocation and prioritise support to activities generating positive externalities, such as basic research and education.

growth_orientation_rationale: Creating special funds for equity capital is expected to enhance the capacity of start-ups to innovate and grow.
status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a major initiative to bolster the startup ecosystem through significant financial support.
description: The government plans to support innovation and entrepreneurship by creating special funds to raise equity capital and enhance the risk-taking capacity of businesses, particularly start-ups and growth firms.
source_quote: Beyond streamlining regulations, the government plans to support innovation and entrepreneurship in several ways. It is creating special funds to raise equity capital and enhance the risk-taking capacity of businesses, notably start-ups and growth firms.

growth_orientation_rationale: Funding key projects in clean technologies, digitalisation, and health is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The funding package has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: This funding package represents a significant investment in priority areas for innovation.
description: The Finnish government has announced a one-off package of €1.6 billion to fund key projects from 2016 to 2018, focusing on clean technologies, digitalisation, and health to encourage innovation.
source_quote: A one-off package of €1.6 billion to fund key projects in 2016-18 will also contribute to encourage innovation, in particular through investments in clean technologies, digitalisation and health.

growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled foreign workers enhances the innovation capacity of the economy and addresses labor shortages in key sectors.
status_evidence: The programme has been actively promoting the employment of international students and researchers.
importance_rationale: The Talent Boost programme represents a significant shift in Finland's approach to immigration and labor market integration, aiming to substantially increase the inflow of skilled workers.
description: The Finnish government launched the Talent Boost programme to attract international talent, introducing measures to facilitate the immigration of highly skilled foreign workers, including a special residence permit for start-ups.
source_quote: Migration policy took a turn in 2017 when the government launched the Talent Boost programme to attract international talent.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance collaboration between businesses and research institutions, which is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially enacted and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: The merger of multiple institutions into a single entity represents a significant structural change in the innovation support system.
description: The Finnish government merged institutions promoting innovation, exports, and investment into Business Finland, a one-stop-shop designed to facilitate network projects that respond to business needs and contribute to financing them, particularly focusing on cooperation between companies and universities.
source_quote: Measures are also being taken to foster entrepreneurship, including from 2018 the merger of institutions promoting innovation, exports and investment into Business Finland.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the financial support for students, potentially increasing their ability to complete education and contribute to the economy.
status_evidence: The proposal is currently being prepared and has not yet been implemented.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the support system for students, moving from grants to loans.
description: A legislative proposal is being prepared to switch from grants to a loan-based support system for living costs for students, raising the maximum guaranteed loan amount and introducing a partial tax allowance for timely course completion.
source_quote: The recent Employment Working Group proposed a switch from grants to a loan-based support system for living costs, making more funding available in the early years of study while strengthening the incentives to complete studies on time.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cooperation and funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and support the growth of start-ups.
status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice as part of the government's ongoing support for R&D.
importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant changes in funding and cooperation mechanisms but do not constitute a major overhaul.
description: Finland aims to enhance cooperation between businesses and universities by using funding criteria for higher-education institutions or R&D vouchers, particularly to support start-ups. The government has created Team Finland to coordinate the activities of several institutions, focusing on internationalisation and maintaining strong government support for basic R&D and education. Additionally, Finland is exploring the conditions for expanding alternative financial instruments for SMEs, including crowdfunding and asset-based lending, to better support innovative firms.

This initiative has led to an increase in joint research projects between industry and universities, enhancing collaboration and innovation capacity. Additionally, a portion of research funds received from industry now stays with universities, allowing contracts to run longer than a fiscal year, which aims to improve collaboration between universities and firms.
source_quote: Since FY2000, a portion of research funds received from industry stays with the university, and contracts can now run longer than a fiscal year.

Additionally, the Council for Science and Technology Policy was established under the Cabinet Office in January 2001 to increase emphasis on basic research and improve the efficiency of public technology spending.

Additionally, Turkey's educational expenditure has declined as a percentage of GDP, raising concerns about the need for improved skills and competences to enhance innovation capacity.
